100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 24, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as coal miners continued their strike, significantly impacting the nation’s economy and prompting discussions about nationalization. In the U.S., the Senate was poised to debate the McNary-Haugen bill aimed at supporting struggling farmers through price stabilization. Meanwhile, in Germany, tensions rose as the nation grappled with hyperinflation and political instability, leading to widespread public unrest.
Updated: Friday, July 24, 2026 at 08:08:22 AM CDT
